## Turn drum audio into a score you can actually edit

## Turn drum audio into a score you can actually edit

Drop in a full mix or an isolated drum stem. Backbeat Forge puts the performance on a five-line drum score you can check against playback and correct by hand. When the chart feels right, export it as a printable PDF or General MIDI file. It all runs locally.

results.title: Have an Inkling
results.content:
Fine-tune it

## Your app has a bug you haven't found

 built Backbeat Forge solo: upload a drum recording, get an editable score, fix what the model got wrong, export. Runs entirely on your machine, so unreleased audio never leaves your drive. [...] Subscribe

1. Home
2. Newsletter
3. The Leaderboard
4. 😸 Have an Inkling

newsletter icon

The Leaderboard

July 20th, 2026

# Have an Inkling

This newsletter was brought to you byFramer 3.0

## Have an Inkling

gm legends, happy Monday.

Today: the former OpenAI CTO ships a model that admits it isn't the strongest, a bot that broke your app before your users could, and a solo dev who figured out how to turn a drum recording into sheet music on your own machine.

## The model built to be changed

, 1M context window, natively multimodal across text, images, and audio, full weights on Hugging Face under Apache 2.0. [...] 🔥 Our Take: There's a specific nightmare where you play something good on the kit and immediately can't figure out what it was. You play it back twenty times and still can't hear the kick pattern. You can do it by ear in a MIDI editor for three hours, or hire someone to transcribe it. Backbeat Forge is the third option: upload the recording, get an editable score, fix what the model missed, export. Hannes built it solo, which shows in the scope. This is specifically for the musician who played something and needs to know what it was. For that person, the transcriptionist just got competition.

Transcribe a take

FROM THE FORUMS

## When bugs don't crash

Love this tool! perfect way to share AI art and AI music. This will be one of my fav tools!

Report [...] On the surface, Turn may seem like a novelty app but when you couple it with something as important as merch, it really proves its worth. If you have a mockup of a vinyl or CD you’re selling, I invite you to upload that image and an audio clip from the release to generate one of these videos. I think you’ll find that Turn’s nod to the physicality of music listening pairs nicely with the marketing of physical products.  
  
www.turn.audio  
  
To celebrate Turn's launch on Product Hunt, we're offering one month of Pro free if you use the code PRODUCTHUNT at checkout. If you're a sound creator, I hope you enjoy the simplicity of Turn and it proves useful in sharing your work.
